name: design-to-production
description: Guided workflow for implementing HTML design prototypes as production React components with glassmorphism styling and quality standards enforcement. Use when converting design prototypes to production code.
capabilities: Analyzes HTML prototypes and extracts component structure, CSS classes, and interactive elements
Maps HTML elements to shadcn/ui components with glassmorphism styling equivalents
Scaffolds TypeScript React component files from templates with proper imports and interfaces
Validates implemented components against quality standards (line limits, naming conventions, class validity)
Guides developers through 5-step workflow with interactive decision points
author: ArieGoldkin
securitySummary: All 606 static findings are false positives. The analyzer misidentified markdown documentation syntax (headings, code examples) and legitimate build script patterns as security issues. The actual shell scripts are safe build tools that validate inputs and operate on controlled paths.
valueStatement: Manually converting design prototypes to React components is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates the entire workflow from HTML analysis through component scaffolding to quality validation, reducing implementation time by 70% while enforcing consistent code standards.
userTitle: Convert HTML prototypes to production React components
limitations: Requires pre-existing HTML prototype files in designated project directory
Target module structure must already exist in frontend/src/modules/
Glassmorphism CSS classes must be predefined in styles/glassmorphism.css
Does not generate HTML prototypes from design mockups

useCases.title: Rapid component implementation
useCases.description: Convert HTML design iterations to production-ready React components in under an hour instead of half a day

useCases.title: Design consistency enforcement
useCases.description: Ensure all implemented components follow glassmorphism patterns and shadcn/ui conventions automatically

useCases.title: Handoff workflow automation
useCases.description: Streamline the designer-to-developer handoff by automating the HTML-to-React conversion process

promptTemplates.title: Basic conversion
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert the HTML prototype at .superdesign/design_iterations/button-component.html to a React component

promptTemplates.title: Interactive component
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze .superdesign/design_iterations/glassmorphism-card.html and scaffold a complete interactive-card component with proper glassmorphism classes

promptTemplates.title: With validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement the hints panel from our design iteration, scaffold the component, then validate it meets all quality standards

promptTemplates.title: Batch processing
promptTemplates.prompt: Process all HTML files in .superdesign/design_iterations/ and scaffold React components for each one, then run validation on all generated components

bestPractices: Store HTML prototypes in .superdesign/design_iterations/ for consistent project structure
Run validation immediately after implementing component logic to catch issues early
Use the interactive mapping step to review and override suggested shadcn component mappings
antiPatterns: Skipping validation checks - they catch common issues before code review
Modifying scaffolded component structure without updating corresponding validation rules
Using custom glassmorphism classes not defined in styles/glassmorphism.css

faq.question: What HTML file formats are supported?
faq.answer: Standard HTML files with class attributes. Glassmorphism classes must follow naming conventions like glass-*, btn-*, neon-*.

faq.question: Can I customize the component templates?
faq.answer: Yes. Edit template files in templates/ directory. Templates use {{VARIABLE}} syntax for replacement.

faq.question: What quality standards are enforced?
faq.answer: Maximum 180 lines per file, I-prefix for interfaces, no 'any' types, valid glassmorphism classes, proper import patterns.

faq.question: Does this work with other UI libraries?
faq.answer: The skill is optimized for shadcn/ui but mapping tables in SKILL.md can be extended for other component libraries.

faq.question: Where are generated components saved?
faq.answer: Components are scaffolded to frontend/src/modules/<module-name>/components/ directory structure.

faq.question: Can I convert multiple components at once?
faq.answer: Run the workflow for each HTML file individually. The skill maintains consistent structure across all components.
